data.body:
# Session handoff — b744 code-review capability + Gerrit voter (2026-07-01)

## TL;DR — what a new session needs to know first
- **b744 code capability is MERGED to `main`** (PR #102, commit `2d3e7611`): WS1–WS6 shipped —
  the four-pass code-review gate AND the Gerrit review-bot rewired to it.
- **Remaining work is LIVE-INFRA + BLOCKED on access this session lacked.** The chain is:
  **WS8 (harden Gerrit auth) → WS7 (make Gerrit the only path into GitHub `main`) → WS6-b (dogfood
  `CONTRIBUTING.md` through Gerrit)**. Do these IN THIS ORDER.
- **DO NOT cut `main` over to Gerrit until auth is hardened.** The live Gerrit
  (`https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com`, 3.14.1) runs `auth.type =
  DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` — anyone can impersonate any account. Locking `main` behind it =
  a security downgrade. The user explicitly chose "harden auth first."

## Done this session (on `main` unless noted)
- WS1–WS5 (already closed before this session): diff assembler + base reviewer; overlay catalog +
  prompts + move-catalog; `overlay_union`/`merge_findings` + `gates/code-review.yaml`;
  `produce_code_review_verdict` + off-by-default shim + single-pass retirement; secrets/security
  fail-closed detectors + CI freshness gate.
- **WS6 (4119, CLOSED)** — reconciled after the d251 merge: instead of a new `src/rebar/gerrit/`
  package (redundant — d251 already shipped `src/rebar/review_bot/`), REWIRED
  `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` over `produce_code_review_verdict`
  (four-pass), fail-closed vote mapping (PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE/inert-disabled/scanner-abstain),
  a `enabled: bool|None` force-enable override added to `produce_code_review_verdict`, a
  coverage-gap-vs-finding message tag, updated `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`. ADR
  `docs/adr/0015-code-review-voter-four-pass-rewire.md` (0013/0014 were taken by d251).
- **WS8 in-repo half (PR #104, was OPEN/auto-merging at handoff — CHECK if merged)**:
  `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md` (the full GitHub-OAuth procedure), a `gerrit.config`
  `[auth]` comment pointer, `gerrit-oauth-provider` provenance in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`.

## Gotchas discovered (save the next session time)
- **`review_code` is now OFF-BY-DEFAULT + FAIL-SAFE** (WS4): it returns a valid (inert-empty when
  disabled / degraded-INDETERMINATE when enabled-without-extra) `review_result` — it NEVER raises.
  Pre-existing tests that asserted "raises without the agents extra" were fixed:
  `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py` (`_FAIL_SAFE` exemption) and
  `tests/interfaces/store/test_snapshot_environments.py` (force-enable the gate). If you touch
  review_code contracts, keep these in sync.
- **Secret-scanning fixture pattern**: the WS5 real-gitleaks test builds its fake `ghp_` PAT at
  RUNTIME (`ghp_` + 36 random chars) so no literal is committed — GitHub push-protection blocks a
  committed literal. Do NOT commit a literal token. (A history rewrite via `git filter-branch` was
  used to purge an earlier committed literal; the original pre-rewrite history is in the local
  backup ref `refs/original/…` if ever needed.)
- **ADR numbering collided** on the d251 merge (duplicate 0007–0013). New ADRs: use 0016+.
- Local dev: always `source .venv/bin/activate`; clear `.rebar/*.cache.json` before a claim
  (stale-cache bug #908b).

## Remaining work — exact next steps
### WS8 (f2d6) — Harden Gerrit auth. **Confirmed strategy: GitHub OAuth via `gerrit-oauth-provider`.**
Follow `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md` verbatim. LIVE steps need:
  1. **navapbc GitHub org-admin** → create an OAuth App, callback
     `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/oauth`; put client-id/secret in SSM (+ add params to
     `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`). (This session's `gh` token lacked `admin:org`.)
  2. **Working AWS access** to the Gerrit host (reached via AWS SSM; this session's AWS session was
     EXPIRED) → install the `oauth.jar` plugin, set `auth.type=OAUTH` + `gitBasicAuthPolicy=HTTP` +
     the `[plugin "gerrit-oauth-provider-github-oauth"]` block, REMOVE become-any-account, restart.
  3. **Provision `rebar-review-bot` an HTTP password BEFORE cutover** (dev-mode required none) so
     the receiver's REST/git access survives. Replication (S5 deploy key) is outbound, unaffected.
  4. Verify: `/config/server/info` → `auth.auth_type == OAUTH`; a human logs in via GitHub; the
     *Become* link is gone; `curl -u rebar-review-bot:<pw> …/a/accounts/self` works.
### WS7 (1bd2) — Make Gerrit the only path into GitHub `main` (CONSUMES d251/S6 tooling).
  - Current GitHub ruleset: `main-protection` (id **18306946**), active. d251/S6 built the
    DeployKey-only ruleset + Terraform (`infra/terraform-github/`) + runbook
    (`infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md`) + a rollback snapshot
    (`infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json`, id 18048287).
  - Remove the legacy protection; leave the S5-deploy-key-only ruleset as the sole `main` gate.
  - Write `CONTRIBUTING.md` (repo root): the Gerrit flow — remote + Change-Id hook +
    `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/main` + the `LLM-Review` vote (finding vs coverage-gap `-1`) +
    amend/re-push + submit + GitHub-is-a-mirror + **how new devs provision Gerrit credentials**
    (the user asked for this explicitly).
  - REQUIRED rollback drill (I have GitHub admin): un-lock via ruleset delete / temp bypass_actor,
    restore the snapshot. Do NOT cut over before WS8 verification passes.
### WS6-b (0da5) — Live dogfood: push `CONTRIBUTING.md` THROUGH Gerrit (`refs/for/main`) → LLM-Review
  vote → submit → replicate to GitHub `main`. Record the change URL + vote on the ticket. This is
  the user's step 3 ("dogfood the cutover").

## Access / identity facts (for the next session)
- GitHub: authed as `JoeOakhartNava`, **admin on navapbc/rebar** (can manage rulesets), token
  scopes `repo, workflow, admin:public_key, read:org, gist` — **no `admin:org`** (can't create the
  org OAuth App via API/UI headlessly).
- AWS: session EXPIRED (`aws sts get-caller-identity` fails). Infra reaches the Gerrit box via SSM.
- Gerrit live + healthy (3.14.1); S5 deploy key `rebar-gerrit-replication` present on the repo.
- Key user directives honored: track work in rebar; PR-merge (auto-merge OK); don't fake live
  steps; harden auth before cutover; document credential provisioning for new devs.
